channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Animal activists celebrate their first global 'Sanctuary Day'
item.description: It's been 40 years since animal advocates founded a sanctuary for farm animals in New York and California, and they say April 17 is their first global sanctuary day.
item.content:encoded: It's been 40 years since animal advocates founded a sanctuary for farm animals in New York and California, and they say April 17 is their first global sanctuary day.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Bottar

item.title: Volleyball player Jordan Lucas on his viral court celebrations
item.description: Volleyball player Jordan Lucas doesn't hide his true self, even when he celebrates on the court.
item.content:encoded: Volleyball player Jordan Lucas doesn't hide his true self, even when he celebrates on the court.
item.dc:creator: Jordan-Marie Smith

item.title: Where art thou?: Mapping Shakespeare's 1613 neighborhood
item.description: The recent discovery of a 17th century map provides new insight into the life of William Shakespeare.
item.content:encoded: The recent discovery of a 17th century map provides new insight into the life of William Shakespeare.
item.dc:creator: Erika Ryan

item.title: As Sri Lanka celebrated its New Year, the war in Iran was felt
item.description: Buddhists and Hindus in Sri Lanka and Hindus marked their New Year on Tuesday. As residents in the capital Colombo celebrated, a war thousands of miles away was making itself felt.
item.content:encoded: Buddhists and Hindus in Sri Lanka and Hindus marked their New Year on Tuesday. As residents in the capital Colombo celebrated, a war thousands of miles away was making itself felt.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: While in a detention cell, a young man heard a voice that allowed him to see hope
item.description: A man recalls the despair he felt when, as a college student, he tried to break into a building on campus and ended up in a detention cell. That's when he heard a voice that helped him find hope.
item.content:encoded: A man recalls the despair he felt when, as a college student, he tried to break into a building on campus and ended up in a detention cell. That's when he heard a voice that helped him find hope.

item.title: Why are oil prices affecting the U.S. if we are a net oil exporter?
item.description: The U.S. produces more oil and gas than any country in the world. So why are prices at the pump 30% higher than they were last year? The Indicator (and brand new Indicator newsletter) explains!
item.content:encoded: The U.S. produces more oil and gas than any country in the world. So why are prices at the pump 30% higher than they were last year? The Indicator (and brand new Indicator newsletter) explains!
item.dc:creator: Cooper Katz McKim

item.title: Historians sue DOJ as Trump shrugs off law meant to preserve White House papers
item.description: The Trump administration asserts a nearly 50-year-old law requiring the preservation of federal government records is unconstitutional. Historians warn important papers could be destroyed.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration asserts a nearly 50-year-old law requiring the preservation of federal government records is unconstitutional. Historians warn important papers could be destroyed.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: New fundraising number show election landscape emerging ahead of primaries
item.description: May and June are big months for primary elections. Already, races we've seen have shown a mixed bag for Republicans while Democrats have often overperformed. And races are driving up big price tags.
item.content:encoded: May and June are big months for primary elections. Already, races we've seen have shown a mixed bag for Republicans while Democrats have often overperformed. And races are driving up big price tags.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: The 2026 NBA Playoffs start Saturday. Here's what to know
item.description: The NBA Playoffs begin Saturday, with the league's two best teams on the same side of the bracket. Meanwhile, for the league's worst teams, an all-time tanking season is finally over.
item.content:encoded: The NBA Playoffs begin Saturday, with the league's two best teams on the same side of the bracket. Meanwhile, for the league's worst teams, an all-time tanking season is finally over.
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Hormuz reopens, but insurers aren't ready to sound the all-clear
item.description: Insurance for cargo and oil vessels stuck in the Strait of Hormuz has skyrocketed. How can insurers help us understand the realities of the war with Iran?
item.content:encoded: Insurance for cargo and oil vessels stuck in the Strait of Hormuz has skyrocketed. How can insurers help us understand the realities of the war with Iran?
item.dc:creator: Avery Keatley
